author: gb9t
content:
Major decisions locked-in 2026-05-09 (user direction):

**1. Q3 ADOPTED: MPROTECT-W→X-block default-on system-wide with per-process JIT exemption**
- New ELF note NT_GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_NEEDS_EXEC_GAIN in .note.gnu.property
- New prctl PR_REQUEST_EXEC_GAIN for runtime self-tagging
- Distros patch JIT runtimes (V8, SpiderMonkey, OpenJDK HotSpot, .NET CLR, BEAM, LuaJIT, PyPy) to set the note
- Implementation homes: fs/exec-binfmt.md (note recognition), kernel/task-lifecycle.md (prctl + state), mm/mmap.md (enforcement)
- ELF note specification: 00-security-principles.md
- Same model applies to NOEXEC-strict
- Transitional period accepted: until distros patch JIT runtimes, JIT-using apps fail at startup

**2. GR-RBAC absorbed as STACKABLE LSM + gradm reimplemented**
- GR-RBAC reimplemented in Rust as a Rookery-original stackable LSM (CONFIG_LSM_GRBAC=y)
- gradm reimplemented as default userspace utility
- Coexists with SELinux/AppArmor (stackable LSM framework, Linux 5.x+)
- GRKERNSEC_* feature flags become policy-loadable settings of GR-RBAC, NOT default-on
- DEFAULT POLICY IS EMPTY/PASSIVE at boot — preserves drop-in compat
- Operators activate via gradm -L (learn) or gradm -E (enforce)
- License: GPL-2.0 reimplementation from open 2018-era grsec patch
- Tier-3 hub: security/grbac/00-overview.md (to be authored)
- Tracked: issue #5 (high, blocked by #1 + #3)

Updates:
- 00-overview.md Foundational Decisions Hardening row: comprehensive summary
- security/00-overview.md: Q3 → D3 resolved; new GR-RBAC LSM row in per-LSM table; full reimplementation rationale documented
- references/grsec-pax-notes.md: row-2 reclassified from 'NOT ABSORBED' to 'ABSORBED as stackable LSM'; design-doc authoring rule revised to route row-2 features to GR-RBAC policy files
- 3 knowledge pages refreshed: 00-overview, security-00-overview, grsec-pax-notes

Open tracking issues:
#2 — 00-security-principles.md (still blocked by #1; preconditions met; ready to author)
#3 — Rookery clippy lints (high, blocking)
#4 — missing rust-for-linux abstractions (medium)
#5 — GR-RBAC + gradm reimplementation (high, NEW; blocked by #1 + #3)

The GR-RBAC absorption substantially expands Rookery's scope — adds ~10K LoC of new Rust kernel code + new userspace tool. This is acceptable per the user's explicit direction. Phase C / Tier-3 design now includes security/grbac/ as a major work item.
